mcqs.question: What type of object is a list in Python?
mcqs.explanation: A list is a mutable object in Python because it can be modified after it's created.

mcqs.question: What type of object is a tuple in Python?
mcqs.explanation: A tuple is an immutable object in Python because it cannot be modified after it's created.

mcqs.question: What happens when you try to modify an immutable object in Python?
mcqs.explanation: When you try to modify an immutable object in Python, you will get a TypeError.

mcqs.question: Why are immutable objects useful in Python?

options.text: Because they can be safely shared between different parts of a program
Because they can be modified in place
Because they are more efficient than mutable objects

mcqs.explanation: Immutable objects are useful in Python because they can be safely shared between different parts of a program.

mcqs.question: Why are mutable objects useful in Python?

options.text: Because they can be safely shared between different parts of a program
Because they can be modified in place
Because they are more efficient than immutable objects

mcqs.explanation: Mutable objects are useful in Python because they can be modified in place, which can be more efficient than creating a new object.

mcqs.question: How can you make a mutable object immutable in Python?

options.text: By converting it to an immutable type

mcqs.explanation: You can make a mutable object immutable in Python by converting it to an immutable type, such as converting a list to a tuple.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of using immutable objects in multithreaded programs?

options.text: They are more efficient than mutable objects
They can be modified in place
They can be safely shared between different parts of a program

mcqs.explanation: Immutable objects are thread-safe, meaning they can be safely accessed and shared by multiple threads.

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of using mutable objects in multithreaded programs?

options.text: They are less efficient than immutable objects
They cannot be modified in place
They cannot be safely shared between different parts of a program

mcqs.explanation: Mutable objects are not thread-safe, meaning they can be modified by multiple threads simultaneously, leading to unexpected behavior.
